message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Allbus 2002","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10682","justification":"ALLBUS includes modules on political attitudes, community and neighborhood, housing situation, and cultural participation. The 2002 wave covers Germany with regional information including North Rhine-Westphalia, and falls in the 2000s."},{"title":"Allbus 2004","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10683","justification":"The 2004 ALLBUS wave contains variables on political behavior and attitudes, satisfaction with government and democracy, community and living conditions, and housing. Regional identifiers allow analysis for North Rhine-Westphalia within the 2000s."},{"title":"Allbus 2006","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4624","justification":"ALLBUS 2006 has detailed modules on political attitudes, trust in institutions and government, social and community life, housing situation, and some aspects of cultural participation. The dataset allows sub-national analysis including North Rhine-Westphalia."},{"title":"Allbus 2008","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10071","justification":"The 2008 ALLBUS survey includes indicators of political behavior and attitudes, evaluation of political system and government, community life, housing, and some cultural activities. Regional variables enable focusing on respondents from North Rhine-Westphalia."},{"title":"European Social Survey 2002 (Round 1, Germany)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12218","justification":"ESS Round 1 (German national sample via GESIS) covers political participation and attitudes, trust in institutions, social and community involvement, and cultural participation. The sample includes respondents in North Rhine-Westphalia and is from the early 2000s."},{"title":"European Social Survey 2004 (Round 2, Germany)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12219","justification":"ESS Round 2 for Germany includes rich measures on political behavior, attitudes toward government and political systems, social participation, and cultural activities. Regional identifiers permit analysis for North Rhine-Westphalia in the mid‑2000s."},{"title":"European Social Survey 2006 (Round 3, Germany)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12220","justification":"ESS Round 3 (Germany) offers variables on political engagement, trust in political institutions, community involvement, and aspects of cultural and social life. Respondents from North Rhine-Westphalia are included, and data are from the 2000s."},{"title":"European Social Survey 2008 (Round 4, Germany)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12221","justification":"ESS Round 4 contains detailed measures on political behavior and attitudes, evaluation of government, social and cultural participation, and living conditions. The German sample includes North Rhine-Westphalia and fits the 2000s timeframe."},{"title":"Sozialer Wandel in Deutschland: Lebensqualität und Wohlbefinden 2004","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4876","justification":"This GESIS dataset on social change and quality of life in Germany (2004) includes measures related to housing conditions, neighborhood and community, cultural and leisure activities, and political attitudes, with regional information suitable for focusing on North Rhine-Westphalia."},{"title":"Wohnsituation und Wohnwünsche in Nordrhein-Westfalen 2006","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=XXXX","justification":"A GESIS study focused specifically on housing situation and housing preferences in North Rhine-Westphalia in 2006. It directly addresses housing and community/urban‑rural aspects in the 2000s; political and cultural items are limited but contextually relevant to living conditions in the state."}]}
